task_name: Mathematical Animation: Matrix as Linear Transformation
description: Create an educational mathematical animation explaining why matrices can perform linear transformations on vectors, showing how matrix columns represent transformed basis vectors and how any vector transforms accordingly.
prompt: I am a math teacher, and I want to create an educational animation for my students to visualize why matrices can perform linear transformations on vectors. Please create an animation as linear_transform.mp4 with at least 5 seconds, showing: 1) A 2D coordinate system with labeled x/y axes; 2) The standard basis vectors $\hat{\imath} = (1,0)$ and $\hat{\jmath} = (0,1)$; 3) A 2x2 transformation matrix $A = \begin{bmatrix} 2 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix}$ and demonstrate how matrix columns represent where basis vectors land after transformation; 4) Show an arbitrary vector $\vec{v} = (1,1)$ and demonstrate that $\vec{v}$ transforms to $A\vec{v}$ by following the transformed basis vectors ($\vec{v} = 1 \cdot \hat{\imath} + 1 \cdot \hat{\jmath}$ becomes $\vec{v}' = 1 \cdot \hat{\imath}' + 1 \cdot \hat{\jmath}'$); 5) Explain visually why this preserves linearity (addition and scalar multiplication).

evaluators.description: Check that the animation video exists
